title: Getting started with maudr
text: maudr generates individualised enzyme kinetics datasets undergraduate teaching. student receives unique Excel file containing simulated absorbance data inhibited non-inhibited enzyme reaction. Instructors can produce per-student answer PDFs plots estimated kinetic parameters. package ships demo data — list four imaginary students kinetic parameters yeast alcohol dehydrogenase (ADH) — can run full workflow without preparing input files. four main functions must used order: initialiseProject() — set folder structure assignReactions() — assign student substrate inhibitor generateAssignments() — produce per-student Excel data files generateAnswers() — produce per-student answer PDFs

title: Getting started with maudr
text: maudr available GitHub :

title: Getting started with maudr
text: initialiseProject() creates self-contained folder structure: default top-level folder named maudr_assignments created current working directory. can changed: initialiseProject() called, project path registered automatically subsequent functions find without needing pass project_path . can still supply project_path explicitly want override .
code: maudr_assignments/   data/   output/     assignments_output/     answers_output/ initialiseProject(path = "~/Desktop") initialiseProject(path = "~/Documents", name = "enzyme_project")

title: Getting started with maudr
text: assignReactions() randomly assigns student substrate inhibitor, writes timestamped metadata file output/assignments_output/.

title: Getting started with maudr
text: input files provided, function uses bundled demo student list (four imaginary students) default ADH parameters:

title: Getting started with maudr
text: Supply student list (optionally) custom enzyme parameters file: student list must contain columns student_no, first_name, surname. enzyme parameters file must contain rxn_substrate, Kcat, Km, Vmax, enzyme_conc, inhibition_actual. input files automatically copied project’s data/ folder. function returns list two elements: $timestamp — string (e.g. "2025-08-22_0848") used identify run later steps $metadata — full student–reaction assignment table

title: Getting started with maudr
text: generateAssignments() reads metadata Step 2 writes one Excel file per student output/assignments_output/<timestamp>/. file contains simulated absorbance vs time data across range substrate concentrations, inhibited uninhibited condition. small, reproducible amount random noise added student’s data default (use_jitter = TRUE), simulating experimental variation. can adjust noise level disable :
code: generateAssignments(run_timestamp = setup$timestamp) generateAssignments(run_timestamp = setup$timestamp, use_jitter = FALSE) generateAssignments(run_timestamp = setup$timestamp, jitter_sd = 0.05)

title: Getting started with maudr
text: generateAnswers() reads student’s Excel file produces: Absorbance vs time plot (faceted condition, coloured substrate concentration) Michaelis-Menten plot (conditions one plot, optional fitted curve) Lineweaver-Burk plot (conditions, fitted regression lines equations) summary table estimated Km Vmax per condition default single combined PDF (answers_all_students.pdf) written output/answers_output/<timestamp>/. Use output_files control :
code: generateAnswers(run_timestamp = setup$timestamp) # One PDF per student generateAnswers(run_timestamp = setup$timestamp, output_files = "separate")  # Both combined and individual files generateAnswers(run_timestamp = setup$timestamp, output_files = "both")

title: Getting started with maudr
text: complete workflow demo mode, using bundled data:
code: library(maudr)  initialiseProject()  setup <- assignReactions()  generateAssignments(run_timestamp = setup$timestamp)  generateAnswers(run_timestamp = setup$timestamp, output_files = "both")

title: Getting started with maudr
text: use enzyme ADH, provide custom enzyme_file required columns. inhibition_actual column must contain least one non-"no_inhibition" value; "no_inhibition" rows used internally represent uninhibited condition generated automatically.

title: Getting started with maudr
text: assignReactions() accepts seed argument (default 1234) controls random assignment students conditions noise added generateAssignments(). Setting seed student list always produce identical assignments data files.

title: maudr
text: maudr R package designed generate, distribute, evaluate enzyme kinetics data teaching assessment undergraduate biochemistry laboratory courses. allows instructors produce individualized, experimental datasets based real enzyme parameters, complete enzyme inhibition conditions, produce corresponding answers visual summaries. package uses yeast alcohol dehydrogenase five alcohol substrates default enzyme, can extended enzyme basic processing parameters known. name package play name Dr Maud Menten, Canadian biochemist established fundamental principles enzyme kinetics, along Dr Leonor Michaelis. typical use scenario package instructors produce individual datasets students contain absorbance values inhibited non-inhibited reaction alcohol dehydrogenase range substrate concentrations different inhibitors. individual datasets analysed students require individual solutions calculated visualised instructors, package also produces plots estimated Km Vmax values individual students’ datasets comparisons inhibited non-inhibited conditions. information package available maudr website vignette.

title: maudr
text: maudr available GitHub, plans upload CRAN.

title: maudr
text: package uses enzyme kinetic parameters S. cerevisiae alcohol dehydrogenase, established 1987 publication Ganzhorn et al.. Excel file parameters ADH inhibitors provided default (reaction_parameters.xlsx), however, parameters enzymes inhibitors can provided user, long column names remain unchanged (Fig. 1.): rxn_substrate Kcat Km Vmax enzyme_conc inhibition_actual default parameters used, input file required package Excel file list students datasets generated analysed. file must one row per student, following column headers (Fig. 2.): student_no – unique student ID (e.g. “u123456”) first_name – student’s first name surname – student’s surname

title: maudr
text: package four main functions produce students’ datasets analysis results; functions used order. meant used order pipeable purpose, enable instructors inspect output step.

title: maudr
text: Sets top-level folder (default location: current folder) input data stored output produced. top-level folder named “maudr_assignments” unless different name specified user. Folders data output created, subfolders output/assignments_output output/answers_output. RStudio project structure used.

title: maudr
text: Takes list students table enzyme parameters creates metadata table assignment every student--reaction conditions, randomly drawn reaction_parameters.xlsx file. table deposited output/assignments_output folder top-level project folder created initialiseProject(). neither input files provided, function runs demo mode, using provided list four imaginary students enzyme parameters ADH proceed. output assignReactions() list timestamp metadata table student-reaction assignments. timestamp used input next function serves unique identifier run package.

title: maudr
text: Takes timestamp assignReactions() input (, invisibly, student-reaction assignments table) produces Excel file student absorbances reactions assigned substrate inhibitor. files deposited output/assignments_output folder given timestamp.

title: maudr
text: Takes timestamp input (, invisibly, student-specific files) produces PDF file results analysis students’ data. results include plots : absorbance vs time (inhibited non-inhibited reactions) Michaelis-Menten curves (inhibited non-inhibited reactions) Lineweaver-Burk (inhibited non-inhibited reactions, regression equations) PDF files can generated individually student separate files, together single file, . PDF files deposited output/assignments_output folder given timestamp.

title: maudr
code: library(maudr)  initialiseProject(path = "~/Desktop")  setup <- assignReactions(student_file = "~/Documents/students_file.xlsx", project_path = "~/Desktop/maudr_assignments") # students_file.xlsx is copied into the data folder in the top level folder; the default reaction_parameters.xlsx is used.  generateAssignments(run_timestamp = setup$timestamp) # Assignments (one Excel file per student) will be deposited in the output/assignment_output folder  generateAnswers(run_timestamp = setup$timestamp, output_files = "both") # Assignments (one PDF file per student plus a single PDf with answers with all students' datasets) will be deposited in the output/answers_output folder

title: maudr
text: Use nls function plot estimate Michaelis-Menten curves inspired 2015 post prof. Paul Brennan describing prof. Rob Benton’s script use . also grateful Fonti Kar helpful suggestions improved package.

title: Summary
text: maudr R package designed generate, distribute, evaluate enzyme kinetics data teaching assessment undergraduate biochemistry laboratory courses. Given list students required input, produces individualized experimental datasets based real enzyme parameters, complete enzyme inhibition conditions, automatically generates corresponding answer PDFs visual summaries. package uses yeast alcohol dehydrogenase five alcohol substrates default, can extended enzyme basic processing parameters known. name package play name Dr Maud Menten, Canadian biochemist established fundamental principles enzyme kinetics, along Dr Leonor Michaelis.

title: Summary
text: Educational resources teaching enzyme kinetics undergraduate courses recently summarised discussed Mak colleagues (2024)). resources tend focus calculations enzymatic transformations (REF) simulations reactions across range kinetic models (REF), less concerned practical logistics providing appropriate datasets large cohorts students ensuring accurate marking work students perform calculations visualise enzyme kinetics. maudr fills gap generating arbitrary number individualised student datasets Excel format, matching data typically acquired “enzyme inhibition” laboratory class, automatically produces visual numerical answers PDF format facilitate marking feedback. package requires basic ability run R functions default provides widely used set kinetic parameters yeast alcohol dehydrogenase inhibitors (REF Ganzhorn). Originally, set scripts became maudr package developed COVID-19 lockdowns, students attend laboratory classes still needed practice enzyme kinetics. Since , scripts routinely used generate datasets solutions formative exercises first- second-year undergradautes, revision sessions contexts, programming classes biology students. One us (JB) presented idea package 2024 useR! conference Salzburg, provided enough encouragement maudr’s development.

title: Summary
text: typical use scenario, instructors provides list students maudr randomly assigns student substrate inhibitor default reaction parameters table, simulates absorbance vs time data inhibited non-inhibited reaction across substrate concentrations 0 160 mM, using enzyme’s Kcat, Km Vmax (REF Ganzhorn). small amount user-controllable random noise added dataset default simulate experimental variation (option can switched ). student receives single Excel file individualised data, information tytpe inhibition hidden. Students expected use file analyse visualise reaction rates absorbance vs time across substrate concentrations, estimate Km Vmax Michaelis-Menten plot identify inhibition type Lineweaver-Burk double-reciprocal plot. individual datasets require solutions calculated visualised instructors check students’ solutions, maudr generates corresponding answers, containing three plots table estimated kinetic parameters expected inhibition type, automatically students’ file. output can saved either one-page PDF per student single collated PDF students’ expected solutions, .

title: Summary
text: Use nls function REF plot estimate Michaelis-Menten curves inspired 2015 post prof. Paul Brennan describing prof. Rob Benton’s script use . also grateful Fonti Kar helpful suggestions improved package.

what: Assign reaction conditions to students — assignReactions
title: Assign reaction conditions to students — assignReactions
text: Randomly assigns student one substrate one inhibition type (excluding "no_inhibition"), creates paired condition "no_inhibition". student therefore two rows: one inhibited one uninhibited reaction. function writes metadata Excel file timestamped assignments_output folder.

title: Assign reaction conditions to students — assignReactions
code: assignReactions(   student_file = NULL,   enzyme_file = NULL,   project_path = getOption("maudr.project_path", "."),   seed = 1234,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Assign reaction conditions to students — assignReactions
text: student_file Optional path Excel file containing student list columns student_no, first_name, surname. enzyme_file Optional path Excel file containing enzyme parameters (e.g. reaction_parameters.xlsx). project_path Path top-level project folder created initialiseProject(). Defaults path registered recent call initialiseProject(), falling back current working directory. seed Random seed reproducibility (default = 1234). verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints progress messages.

title: Assign reaction conditions to students — assignReactions
text: Invisibly returns list : timestamp Timestamp string identifying output folder. metadata tibble student–reaction assignments.

title: Assign reaction conditions to students — assignReactions
text: input files provided, demo datasets bundled package used automatically.

title: Assign reaction conditions to students — assignReactions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ proj <- initialiseProject() assignReactions(project_path = proj, seed = 4321) } # }

what: Generate answer PDFs from assignment Excel files — generateAnswers
title: Generate answer PDFs from assignment Excel files — generateAnswers
text: Reads student assignment files, estimates rates (optionally) Km/Vmax, renders per-student answer pages : Absorbance vs time (faceted rxn_condition) Michaelis-Menten (conditions one plot; optional nls fit) Lineweaver-Burke (conditions one plot; fitted lm lines)

title: Generate answer PDFs from assignment Excel files — generateAnswers
code: generateAnswers(   run_timestamp,   output_files = c("single", "separate", "both"),   project_path = getOption("maudr.project_path", "."),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Generate answer PDFs from assignment Excel files — generateAnswers
text: run_timestamp Timestamp string (YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM) assignReactions(). output_files One "separate", "single", "". Default "single". project_path Path top-level project folder (defaults path registered initialiseProject(), falling back "."). verbose Logical; show progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Generate answer PDFs from assignment Excel files — generateAnswers
text: Invisibly TRUE.

what: Generate assignment Excel files for each student — generateAssignments
title: Generate assignment Excel files for each student — generateAssignments
text: Reads setup metadata (assignReactions()) produces per-student Excel files simulated absorbance vs. time data. student receives inhibited uninhibited conditions one sheet. true inhibition type remains instructor metadata.

title: Generate assignment Excel files for each student — generateAssignments
code: generateAssignments(   run_timestamp,   project_path = getOption("maudr.project_path", "."),   verbose = TRUE,   cuv_vol = 0.003,   eps = 6220,   enz_vol = 0.1,   substr_conc = c(0, 10, 20, 40, 80, 160),   time_vec = c(0.17, 0.33, 0.5, 0.66, 0.83, 1),   use_jitter = TRUE,   jitter_sd = 0.02 )

title: Generate assignment Excel files for each student — generateAssignments
text: run_timestamp Timestamp string (YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM) assignReactions(). project_path Path top-level project folder (defaults path registered initialiseProject(), falling back "."). verbose Logical; show progress messages (default TRUE). cuv_vol Cuvette volume litres (default 0.003). eps Extinction coefficient (default 6220). enz_vol Enzyme volume millilitres (default 0.1). substr_conc Vector substrate concentrations mM. time_vec Vector reaction time points minutes. use_jitter Logical; add random noise absorbances (default TRUE). jitter_sd Numeric; SD additive noise use_jitter = TRUE (default 0.02, .e. ±2% variability).

title: Generate assignment Excel files for each student — generateAssignments
text: Invisibly returns TRUE writing student assignment files updating metadata.

title: Generate assignment Excel files for each student — generateAssignments
text: Random measurement noise ("jitter") optionally applied per student reproducible way based run seed student ID.

what: Initialise a maudr project folder structure — initialiseProject
title: Initialise a maudr project folder structure — initialiseProject
text: Creates self-contained top-level project folder (default: "maudr_assignments") containing subfolders data/, code/, output/ (assignments_output/ answers_output/ inside). Works RStudio Positron; .Rproj file created.

title: Initialise a maudr project folder structure — initialiseProject
Initialise a maudr project folder structure — initialiseProject
text: path Base directory create project folder (default "."). name Name top-level project folder (default "maudr_assignments"). verbose Logical; show progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Initialise a maudr project folder structure — initialiseProject
text: full path newly created project folder (invisibly).

title: Initialise a maudr project folder structure — initialiseProject
